message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports — on EVERY axis.
The operator's directive: use ALL scorers; we must win on all, not cherry-pick the one we pass.

WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS.
Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md — ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped,
Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist. NO FS-Researcher artifact (a background search is hunting the
FS-Researcher paper+benchmark separately) — report FSR as an open gap, never fake a third column.
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md (~35 exist).

THE THREE AXES (orthogonal — a report can win one and lose another):
- DeepTRACE (EXISTS): scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py — 8 citation-faithfulness metrics.
  Judge lock config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge=moonshotai/kimi-k2.6,
  self_rescore fairness policy). Preflight deeptrace_judge_preflight.py HARD-BLOCKS if OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  is unset or provider headroom < 8. The key IS available: source it with
  `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a` (NEVER print it).
- RACE (DOES NOT EXIST — must build): DeepResearch-Bench 4-dim reference-based LLM judge —
  Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by per-task criteria
  vs a reference answer. Build it honestly; do NOT relabel DeepTRACE as RACE.
- DRB-II (EXTERNAL): scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py adapts the official DeepResearch-Bench-II
  rubric harness, but third_party/ is absent. Fetch it if fetchable; if not, say so plainly.

HARD HONESTY RULES:
- If POLARIS LOSES on an axis, say so and by how much. A flattering scoreboard is worse than none.
- Never tune a judge to make us win. Score every system with the SAME judge/tasks (self_rescore).
- Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score the ~35 reports that already exist.
- Cost/rate: paid judge calls on a SHARED OpenRouter account (~217 credits left). If you see HTTP 429,
  SAY SO in blockers; do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


YOU ARE FABLE, THE INDEPENDENT GATE. Build the honest multi-axis scoreboard.

DEEPTRACE (faithfulness): {
  "axis": "DeepTRACE (citation faithfulness) — Axis 1",
  "ran": false,
  "built": false,
  "polaris_score": "unmeasured — no 8-metric number was produced. The scorer CAN score POLARIS (drb_72 report parses to 259 bibliography entries / 415 statements / 245 [N] citations, and corpus_snapshot.json supplies source spans for the kimi support matrix), but a POLARIS-only number is non-comparable under the signed lock's self_rescore policy (which REQUIRES every competitor scored by the same judge) and cannot answer the axis question \"does POLARIS beat the competitors.\" Deliberately did NOT spend shared credits on a number that cannot establish a win. Preflight is GREEN (kimi-k2.6, 21 operational providers >= floor 8, lock signed, exit 0), so a number is *claimable* the moment comparable competitor inputs exist.",
  "competitor_scores": "unmeasured for all 4 (ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped, Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped) — cannot be scored comparably with current assets. DeepTRACE needs a Factual-Support Matrix over each cited source's FETCHED content plus a parseable citation->source map. VERIFIED offline ($0) with the repo's own parser: all 4 competitor artifacts yield 0 bibliography entries and 0 real [N] citations (ChatGPT_Scoped 0 bib / 9 false-positive bracket matches; ChatGPT_Unscoped 0/0; Gemini_Scoped 0/0; Gemini_Unscoped 0/0), vs POLARIS 259 bib / 245 cites. ChatGPT cites via opaque unicode `citeturn...view...` UI tokens (no bibliography, unresolvable to fetchable sources); Gemini has a Works-cited list (~32 URLs) but superscript markers that don't map to [N] and no banked source text. No fetched source content exists for ANY competitor, and the third_party/answer-engine-eval scraper DeepTRACE uses to fetch it is absent. Feeding the .md as-is produces all-zero matrices = a fake, POLARIS-flattering scoreboard, which the honesty rules forbid. FSR: no artifact exists (open gap; no third column fabricated).",
  "polaris_wins": "unmeasured",
  "margins": "n/a — no per-dimension margins computable. A same-judge self_rescore comparison could not be produced: POLARIS is scorable but the 4 competitor artifacts cannot be turned into a support matrix (no fetched source content; ChatGPT citations unresolvable in principle). No metric-by-metric POLARIS-vs-competitor deltas exist to sign.",
  "command": "set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a; python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check   # -> PASS: kimi-k2.6, 21 providers (floor 8), signed, self_rescore, exit 0. Then offline parser proof (scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py: parse_bibliography/split_statements/citation_columns) over /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md + POLARIS drb_72 report.md -> competitors 0 bib/0 real cites, POLARIS 259 bib/245 cites.",
  "competitor_scores_note": "n/a",
  "polaris_score_note": "n/a",
  "blockers": [
    "SCORING BLOCKER (CONFIRMED, offline $0): the 4 competitor artifacts carry no [N]+bibliography structure and no fetched source content, so the DeepTRACE Factual-Support Matrix cannot be built for them with the same kimi judge. Parser proof: ChatGPT_Scoped 0 bib/9 false-positive brackets, ChatGPT_Unscoped 0/0, Gemini_Scoped 0/0, Gemini_Unscoped 0/0; POLARIS drb_72 259 bib/245 cites.",
    "ChatGPT_Scoped/Unscoped cite via opaque unicode `citeturn...view...` UI tokens (private-use separators, no bibliography) that are UNRESOLVABLE to fetchable sources -> scoring these two on faithfulness is impossible in principle without their original browsing session, not merely hard.",
    "third_party/answer-engine-eval (the DeepTRACE source-scraper + gpt-4o statement extractor) is ABSENT from the repo, so even Gemini's ~32 Works-cited URLs cannot be fetched+judged through the intended harness without a live-scraping rebuild.",
    "A same-judge self_rescore comparison (required by the signed lock for any DeepTRACE claim) is therefore unachievable with present assets; a POLARIS-only number would be non-comparable, so none was purchased (preserving the shared ~217-credit budget).",
    "No 429s encountered (only the offline parser + a single preflight GET to OpenRouter ran; no paid judge calls were spent).",
    "FS-Researcher: no artifact present — open gap, third column not fabricated."
  ],
  "raw": "STEP 1 PREFLIGHT (RAN, PASS): `python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check` after `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a` ->\n  benchmark judge: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 | lock signed: True | operational providers: 21 (floor 8) | comparability policy: self_rescore | OK - a DeepTRACE number is claimable under this lock. | EXIT=0.\nThe earlier hard-block was solely the unset OPENROUTER_API_KEY (now present, len 73). Key never printed.\n\nSTEP 2 PARSER PROOF (RAN offline, $0; scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py parse funcs):\n  system            bib[N]  stmts  cited_stmts  tot_[N]cites\n  ChatGPT_Scoped      0     2000      9(fp)        9(fp)\n  ChatGPT_Unscoped    0      162      0            0\n  Gemini_Scoped       0      655      0            0\n  Gemini_Unscoped     0      320      0            0\n  POLARIS_drb72     259      415    200          245\nThe DeepTRACE scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, compute_deeptrace_metrics) is a PURE C x S metric function; it needs a citation matrix C and a judge-built support matrix S. Only POLARIS has the inputs (parseable [N]+## Bibliography at report.md:783, plus corpus_snapshot.json 13MB source spans, plus 409 banked kimi verdicts in four_role_settled_verdicts.jsonl). Competitors have neither a parseable citation structure nor fetched source content.\n\nCITATION-FORMAT EVIDENCE:\n  ChatGPT: `citeturn24view0turn25view0...` opaque UI tokens (cat -v shows M-nM-^HM-^B private-use separators); 0 bibliography; only ~25 stray URLs in the whole doc; tokens do not resolve to sources.\n  Gemini: `#### Works cited` list with ~32 real URLs (e.g. arxiv.org/pdf/2304.11771) but inline superscript citation markers that don't parse as [N], and no banked source text.\n\nHONESTY POSTURE: running the scorer on the competitor .md as-is would emit all-zero citation/support matrices -> unsupported_ratio 1.0, citation accuracy/thoroughness 0.0, i.e. a scoreboard that fakes a POLARIS blowout for a purely structural reason (we lack their sources), not a faithfulness reason. Per the directive (\"a flattering scoreboard is worse than none\"; \"score every system with the SAME judge/tasks\"), that number was NOT produced. Files: /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py, /workspace/POLARIS/config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py, /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md, /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/paid_drb72_deep/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/."
}
RACE (quality):          null
DRB-II (rubric):         null

Produce the operator's real answer:
1. A single scoreboard: for EACH axis (DeepTRACE, RACE, DRB-II), does POLARIS beat ChatGPT? beat
   Gemini? — yes / no / partial / unmeasured, with the margin. Mark clearly which numbers are REAL
   (actually computed) vs unmeasured/blocked.
2. The blunt bottom line: on how many of the three axes can we HONESTLY claim to beat both competitors
   right now? Where do we LOSE, and by how much?
3. FS-Researcher: state the gap honestly (no artifact yet).
4. The single highest-leverage next action to make "beats all, on all axes" a defensible claim.
Do not flatter. The operator has been burned by a confident wrong thesis before.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: You've hit your session limit · resets 6:40pm (UTC)
